mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is Kafka API compatible and designed for simplicity and speed. It eliminates the need for ZooKeeper and JVM, making it a lightweight alternative for developers. Ideal for real-time data processing, Redpanda is suitable for applications requiring low-latency and high-throughput messaging.

steps.title: Add the Redpanda Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions necessary for Redpanda to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

> This command installs the Redpanda CRDs.

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Redpanda in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

> This command deploys Redpanda in the 'redpanda' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Redpanda cluster by applying a Custom Resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

```

> This command creates a Redpanda cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> This command lists the Redpanda clusters and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> This command uninstalls the Redpanda release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda -o yaml > backup-redpanda.yaml

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> Backup the current Redpanda configuration.
> This command upgrades Redpanda to the specified version.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the status of the Redpanda cluster.
commands: kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda -o yaml > backup-redpanda.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda service is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redpanda pods with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in redpanda namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda cluster has not been created or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Redpanda cluster was created successfully and check the namespace spelling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to accommodate the Redpanda pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on Redpanda pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources allocated to the pods.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redpanda' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for topic partitioning and added support for new Kafka API features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redpanda cluster listed with 'kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda' showing the status as 'Ready'.

kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
